creator: jhs@berklix.com
text:
Hi Jeremy + CC,
Ref. Both:
> -----------
> From: "Jeremy Messenger" <mezz7@cox.net>
> Date: Sat, 12 Dec 2009 12:54:03 -0600 (19:54 CET)
> To: bug-followup@freebsd.org, jhs@berklix.com
> 
> The correct way is to add a patch to get it installs in  
> ${PREFIX}/libdata/pkgconfig/. If it installs in the lib/pkgconfig/ then  
> it's a bug. I will check it this evening or tomorrow and try to fix it.
> -----------
> From: mezz@freebsd.org
> Date: Sun, 13 Dec 2009 03:59:56 GMT (04:59 CET)
> To: jhs@berklix.com, mezz@freebsd.org, gnome@freebsd.org
> 
> Synopsis: math/gnumeric/pkg-plist
> 
> State-Changed-From-To: open->closed
> State-Changed-By: mezz
> State-Changed-When: Sun Dec 13 03:58:32 UTC 2009
> State-Changed-Why: 
> Your ports tree is not up to date. Currently, we have gnumeric-1.9.16 and
> pointyhat does not has any problem to create package of it. In my system:
> 
> # find /usr/local -name libspreadsheet-\*.pc
> /usr/local/libdata/pkgconfig/libspreadsheet-1.10.pc
> 
> http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=141306
> -----------

8.0-RELEASE & what was then current were both broken when I tested,
but latest current now OK here too.  Thanks Jeremy.

It was unfortunate "make package" broke in 8.0-RELEASE.  Presumably
not detected by cluster build scripts not catching exit != 0 as
concentrating on building maximum packages regardless of failures.
I'll hope over holidays to read 
	http://www.freebsd.org/doc/en/articles/portbuild/

Cheers,
Julian
-- 
Julian Stacey: BSD Unix Linux C Sys Eng Consultants Munich http://berklix.com
Mail plain text not quoted-printable, HTML or Base64:  http://asciiribbon.org
